name: Bagua Mirror
description: The Bagua Mirror (八卦鏡, Bāguà jìng) is a potent feng shui tool and a significant object in Chinese mythology and tradition. It is typically a circular mirror surrounded by an octagonal frame, each side of which is adorned with one of the eight trigrams (八卦, Bāguà) representing the fundamental principles of reality. These trigrams, derived from the I Ching (Book of Changes), symbolize various aspects of the cosmos, including heaven, earth, thunder, wind, water, fire, mountain, and lake. The Bagua Mirror is believed to possess the power to deflect negative energy (sha qi), ward off evil spirits, and protect homes and businesses from harmful influences. It is often placed above doorways or windows facing potential sources of negative energy, such as sharp corners of buildings, roads pointing directly at the dwelling, or cemeteries. The mirror works by reflecting the negative energy away, neutralizing its harmful effects. There are different types of Bagua Mirrors, including concave, convex, and flat mirrors. Concave mirrors are used to absorb and redirect positive energy, while convex mirrors are used to reflect and disperse negative energy. Flat mirrors offer a more balanced approach, reflecting both positive and negative energy. The proper placement and type of Bagua Mirror are crucial for its effectiveness and should be determined by a knowledgeable feng shui practitioner. The Bagua Mirror is not intended to be used offensively or to harm others; its primary purpose is to protect and harmonize the environment.
subtitle: Deflects negative energy, wards off evil.
material: Mirror (glass or metal), wood or metal frame
origin: Derived from the principles of the I Ching and feng shui.
currentLocation: Homes, businesses, temples across the world.
associatedDeities: None directly, but aligns with Taoist principles
associatedHeroes: None directly, used by practitioners of feng shui

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Bagua
keyMyths.description: Legend says that the Bagua was revealed to the mythical Emperor Fu Xi, who used it to understand the patterns of the universe. This understanding led to the development of the I Ching.

keyMyths.title: Bagua Mirror and Feng Shui
keyMyths.description: The Bagua Mirror's effectiveness relies on the principles of Feng Shui, aiming to create harmony by balancing positive and negative energies in the environment. It is used to correct imbalances and protect against harmful influences.

sources.author: Eva Wong
sources.work: Feng Shui: The Ancient Wisdom of Harmonious Living for Modern Times
sources.description: Provides information on the use of Bagua Mirrors in feng shui.
sources.text: Provides information on the use of Bagua Mirrors in feng shui.

sources.author: Derek Walters
